name: understand-diff
tagline: Maps a git diff's changed files to affected components/layers via a local knowledge-graph JSON, for risk assessment.
verdict: Clean, fully local skill: reads git diff/log output and a project-generated .ua/knowledge-graph.json, then writes a diff-overlay.json for a dashboard. No credential access, no network egress, no piped remote code, no destructive git ops, nothing hidden. Only soft gaps: no explicit negative-trigger guidance and no version/changelog signal in the frontmatter —

highlights.summary: No explicit 'do not use when...' guidance (e.g., when no knowledge graph exists yet, beyond the runtime check in step 1); minor clarity gap, not a safety issue.
No version field or changelog/change-signal present in frontmatter or body; harmless but makes it harder for a consumer to detect updates.
